summary:
Thin cycle: only 7 of 60 tracked tweets carried a bank/strategist tag; no @neilksethi / @neilksethinew posts appeared in this window. Citadel's Rubner dominates with 4 charts (relayed by @MikeZaccardi) on surging 0DTE options activity, record May/June retail trading volumes, broadening household equity ownership, and SPX dispersion at the 99th percentile. Goldman's JOLTS-based jobs-workers gap estimate fell to 0.0mn in June. BofA flagged Energy and Financials as the highest total-shareholder-yield sectors. Wells Fargo projected 22% S&P 500 Q2 earnings growth on AI-driven capex.

Bank counts: Citadel 4, BofA 1, Goldman 1, Wells Fargo 1.

banks.theme: Rubner (Citadel) — 0DTE options surge, record retail participation, broadening equity ownership, extreme SPX dispersion

items.author: MikeZaccardi
items.text:
Rubner Citadel: The fastest growth in equity ownership is occurring among households that historically had the lowest market participation rates. 

Today, the bottom 50% of US households own more than $615 billion of equities and mutual funds, a record high. https://t.co/AIxAy6Wrgn
